entries.title: Republican lawmakers showed signs Tuesday they were getting nervous about the course of the Iran war and the economic impact on voters ahead of the November elections.
Anthropic filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration for designating the artificial-intelligence company a security threat and trying to cancel its federal contracts
As events shift in Iran so does the White House messaging about the war. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics Newsletter:
Each side in the Iran war seems to have miscalculated how the other would behave, triggering an ever-expanding conflict with few clear ways out in the foreseeable future
Who will Trump pick as MAGA heir for the ’28 election? He has lately extolled Rubio amid the focus on foreign policy, but many see Vance as a better fit.
Lawrence Summers has lost his affiliation with America’s leading economics organization, the latest consequence for the former Treasury secretary stemming from his ties to the late Jeffrey Epstein
President Trump is under pressure to address surging gas prices, stretched-thin munitions and sustained opposition to the war
How the WSJ Tracked Claims of Assault Against Immigration Agents
Few people lobbied President Trump to intervene militarily in Iran as effectively as the hawkish and persistent Sen. Lindsey Graham
Trump wants Iran’s unconditional surrender, a DOJ official goes after Cuban leaders, and RFK Jr. angers Boston. Read more in today’s What’s News newsletter:
Customs and Border Protection said a system for tariff refunds will be operational in 45 days
Some Republicans complain Virginia’s most prominent GOP figure is missing from their counterattacks against Democrats’ plan to redraw the state’s congressional maps
Rep. Tony Gonzales (R., Texas) said he wouldn’t seek re-election. The announcement came as House GOP leaders asked the lawmaker to quit his campaign after he admitted to an affair.
Rahm Emanuel is delivering the Democratic Party a dose of tough medicine—in his usual blunt style—as the party enters a critical midterm primary season
Sen. Steve Daines of Montana, a close ally of President Trump, said he wouldn’t run for re-election this year, formally exiting the race shortly before the filing deadline for candidates in his state
Democrat James Talarico’s victory Tuesday has spooked Republicans who fear they could lose a U.S. Senate seat in November
Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. are backing Powerus, a drone company vying to meet Pentagon demand and fill a hole left by the administration’s ban on new Chinese drones in the U.S.

U.S. investigators believe hackers affiliated with the Chinese government are responsible for a cyber intrusion on an internal FBI computer network that holds information related to some domestic surveillance orders
A federal prosecutor is looking to build criminal cases against leaders of Cuba’s government and Communist Party, as Trump has openly suggested the country would be his next target for regime change
As conservative critics question the assault on Iran, President Trump’s TV-ready defense secretary is going on offense
Pentagon officials are drawing up plans to replenish U.S. munitions expended fighting Iran over the past week, according to people familiar with the matter
